title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.
writeup: A full-course walkthrough of building production mobile apps with Claude Code, starting from zero and ending with App Store and Google Play submissions. The instructor, who runs a $300K/month business built almost entirely on Claude Code, uses a habit tracker as the pedagogical vehicle because it exercises the full stack: local storage, external APIs, a database, cloud hosting, and authentication. The core method is web-first development (design and iterate in a browser, then port to iOS and Android via React Native and Expo), which keeps the feedback loop fast and the toolchain accessible to anyone who can type in a terminal.
key_points: The recommended development pattern is to build and iterate as a web application first, then port to mobile using React Native and Expo, which avoids the slower native emulator cycle and lets the same codebase target both the web and phone.

The course follows a deliberate complexity ladder: local-only (no API, no database, data in local storage), then add a third-party API, then a cloud database, then authentication, mirroring the architecture of Cal AI, a calorie-tracking app that sold for $50 to $100 million to My Fitness Pal.

Claude Code can be accessed through the claude.ai web app, the official desktop app, or a third-party IDE such as Anti-Gravity (a Google product) or VS Code with the Claude Code extension; the instructor uses Anti-Gravity plus the Claude Code for VS Code extension for file-level granularity.

The Claude Pro plan at $20/month is sufficient to follow the course; the instructor's own Max plan, costing several hundred dollars per month, is the backbone of a business generating $300,000 per month in revenue, cited as evidence of the ROI ceiling.

A habit tracker app scaffolded with Claude Code and a small set of prompt templates was completed in under 5 minutes, demonstrating that the bottleneck is prompt construction and iteration strategy, not raw coding speed.
